summary: First iteration end-to-end smoke + integration test for BookTalent. Backend: 28/28 pytest tests pass (auth, artists, packages CRUD, full booking state machine — create → pay → accept → complete → approve → wallet credit, reviews, withdrawals, media, availability blocking, KYC, admin stats/lists/coupons). Frontend: landing, login (/login route — note: spec referenced /signin but actual route is /login), signup, search → artist profile (all 4 tabs), and dashboards for customer/admin/artist all load with every expected sidebar data-testid present. Token stored in localStorage under key 'bt_token'.

minor.issue: Requires full PackageBody (not a partial PATCH). Sending only {price} returns 422. Consider using a PartialPackage model or switching to PATCH.
Currently auth-protected (Depends on get_current_user). For a public checkout page this should be open OR clearly documented. Test had to send admin token.
Field `guests` typed as Optional[str] — frontend integers will 422. Recommend Optional[int] or coerce.

ui_bugs.issue: React hydration error: <span> cannot be a child of <option>. The package <select>'s <option> wraps a styled span. Browsers strip non-text inside <option> and React warns.
Page title is 'Emergent | Fullstack App' across all routes — should be branded e.g. 'BookTalent — Book India's Finest Talent'.

action_items: Fix <option> hydration warning in ArtistProfile package selector (use plain text in <option>, do styling via parent <select> or custom dropdown).
Consider supporting partial update on PUT /api/packages/{id} OR move to PATCH.
Decide whether /api/coupons/validate should be public; if yes, drop the auth dependency.
Optional: change BookingCreate.guests to int with coercion.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1,766 lines — should be split into routers (auth, artists, bookings, payments, wallet, admin, etc.) for maintainability.

Several endpoints set 'expires_at' to utcnow() with no real TTL (OTP and password resets) — they appear to be intended to expire but stored expiry equals creation time. Bug risk; right now OTP only checks value == 123456 so it works in mock, but the schema is misleading.
Many MongoDB queries fetch full collections (reviews, bookings.to_list(500), notifications.to_list(50)) without indexes — fine for demo but flag for production.
Token uses long expiry (7 days?) — make sure refresh logic exists for production.
Frontend uses single bt_token in localStorage (no httpOnly cookie) — acceptable per spec but worth noting from a security perspective.
<option> contains <span> in ArtistProfile.jsx (~line 219) — invalid HTML and produces hydration warnings.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% — all sidebars, all auth flows, all dashboards loaded; one HTML/hydration warning

test_credentials: Admin admin@booktalent.com/Admin@123, Customer customer@booktalent.com/Customer@123, Artist priya@booktalent.com/Artist@123. Mock OTP 123456. Sign-in route is /login (NOT /signin). Token key in localStorage is 'bt_token'.
seed_data_creation: Test created a new customer (TEST_cust_<uuid>@booktalent.com / Test@1234), one TEST_PKG package (deleted at end), several bookings on seed artist priya, blocked availability date 2028-01-15 on priya's calendar, and one TESTCPN<hex> coupon.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend route /login (not /signin) is the sign-in screen. localStorage key is bt_token. Booking guests field is a string. Coupon validate requires auth. Full booking flow can be exercised end-to-end via the test_credentials supplied. backend_test.py is reusable as regression.
